---
title: "Quest 19: Structs and Impl - Rust Blueprints"
subtitle: "Model Data and Methods with Structs"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🏗️ QUEST 19 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 10 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/19-classes.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/19-classes.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Rust uses `struct` + `impl` where Python uses classes.
You can think of this as a clear data blueprint plus methods.

## 💡 Struct Example

```rust
struct Hero {
    name: String,
    hp: i32,
}

impl Hero {
    fn describe(&self) {
        println!("{} has {} HP", self.name, self.hp);
    }

    fn heal(&mut self, amount: i32) {
        self.hp += amount;
    }
}

fn main() {
    let mut hero = Hero {
        name: String::from("Luna"),
        hp: 50,
    };

    hero.describe();
    hero.heal(10);
    hero.describe();
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Create a `Book` struct with `title` and `pages`, then print one book.
If that works, add one extra method such as `is_long()`.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
struct Book {
    title: String,
    pages: u32,
}

impl Book {
    fn show(&self) {
        println!("{} ({} pages)", self.title, self.pages);
    }
}

fn main() {
    let book = Book {
        title: String::from("Rust for Starters"),
        pages: 120,
    };
    book.show();
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 19 Complete
You completed the full Rust Quest sequence.
Fantastic work—keep building small Rust projects to reinforce these ideas.
:::
